candidate_problems.text: What principled criterion distinguishes a genuinely agential 'reasons-responsive causal architecture' from any sufficiently complex deterministic control system? The conjecture shifts the debate from metaphysical freedom to authorship, but leaves open where the boundary lies and how to avoid trivializing free will by attributing it to thermostats, algorithms, or institutions.

Are the first-person practical standpoint and the third-person physical standpoint merely complementary levels of description, or can they generate genuinely conflicting claims about the same action? The conjecture assumes explanatory pluralism resolves the contradiction, but it remains open whether this is a stable reconciliation or just a relabeling that leaves the core incompatibilist challenge untouched.

How, exactly, do responsibility practices and deliberative institutions causally improve behavior, and would abandoning free-will language actually degrade those feedback loops? The conjecture asserts a social-functional cost to eliminativism, but this needs a testable account of the mechanisms by which treating people as choice-capable agents changes outcomes versus alternative deterministic framings.
